title: Discord Integration Guide
content:
## Overview

Discord is a communication platform that provides text, voice, and video channels organized into servers. While originally popular in gaming communities, Discord has been adopted by many technology teams, startups, and security communities for real-time collaboration. The T1 Agentics integration with Discord enables SOC teams to send security alerts and notifications to Discord channels via webhooks or bot connections, facilitating rapid communication during security incidents.

**Vendor:** Discord
**Category:** Communication & Collaboration
**Authentication:** Bot Token or Webhook URL

## Prerequisites

Before configuring the Discord integration in T1 Agentics, ensure you have:

- A Discord server (guild) with administrative or Manage Webhooks permissions
- For Bot integration (full functionality):
  - A Discord Application created at [https://discord.com/developers/applications](https://discord.com/developers/applications)
  - A Bot created within the application
  - The **Bot Token** from the Bot settings page
  - Required bot permissions (set via OAuth2 URL Generator):
    - `Send Messages`
    - `Manage Channels` (for creating incident channels)
    - `Embed Links` (for rich message formatting)
    - `Attach Files` (for uploading reports)
    - `Read Message History`
  - The bot must be invited to the target Discord server
- For Webhook integration (simpler, send-only):
  - A webhook created in the target channel: **Channel Settings > Integrations > Webhooks > New Webhook**
  - Copy the **Webhook URL**
- Network access from T1 Agentics to the Discord API (`https://discord.com/api`)

## Configuration in T1 Agentics

1. Navigate to **Connect > Marketplace** in the T1 Agentics sidebar
2. Search for **Discord** or browse the **Communication** category
3. Click on the Discord card and select **Configure**
4. Enter your connection details:
   - **Connection Name**: A descriptive label (e.g., "Discord SOC Server")
   - **Integration Method**: Select Bot or Webhook
   - For Bot:
     - **Bot Token**: Your Discord bot token
     - **Default Server ID**: The Discord server (guild) ID
     - **Default Channel ID**: The default channel for notifications
   - For Webhook:
     - **Webhook URL**: The Discord webhook URL
5. Click **Test Connection** to verify access
6. Click **Save** to activate the integration
7. The integration will appear in your **Connect > Active Integrations** list

## Available Actions

| Action | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| Send Message | Generic | Post a message to a Discord channel |
| Send Webhook Message | Generic | Send a message via webhook (with embeds) |
| Send Embed | Generic | Send a rich embed message with fields, colors, and formatting |
| Create Channel | Generic | Create a new text channel in the server |
| Delete Channel | Generic | Delete a channel (for cleanup after incidents) |
| Set Channel Topic | Generic | Update a channel's topic description |
| List Channels | Investigate | List all channels in the server |
| List Server Members | Investigate | List members of the Discord server |
| Send Direct Message | Generic | Send a direct message to a specific user |
| Upload File | Generic | Upload a file to a channel |

## Common Use Cases

- **Security alert notifications**: Send formatted security alerts with rich embeds (color-coded by severity, with IOC details and action links) to a dedicated Discord channel
- **Incident communication**: Create temporary Discord channels for incident coordination, especially useful for teams that use Discord as their primary communication platform
- **Community security advisories**: For organizations with Discord-based communities, send security advisories and status updates to relevant channels
- **Webhook-based alerting**: Use simple webhook integration for quick setup of alert notifications without the overhead of a full bot application
- **SOC team collaboration**: Facilitate real-time discussion during security investigations through dedicated Discord channels

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| Bot token invalid | Verify the token is correct and the bot has not been deleted or regenerated. Reset the token in the Discord Developer Portal if needed |
| Missing permissions | The bot lacks required permissions in the target server or channel. Check the role hierarchy and channel permission overrides |
| Webhook returns 404 | The webhook may have been deleted. Recreate the webhook in the target channel's integration settings |
| Message not appearing | For bots, verify the bot is in the server and has access to the target channel. For webhooks, verify the webhook URL is complete and correct |
| Rate limiting (429) | Discord has strict rate limits (5 requests per second per endpoint). Implement rate limiting and respect the `Retry-After` header |
| Embed formatting issues | Discord embeds have specific limits: title (256 chars), description (4096 chars), fields (25 max). Ensure your content fits within these limits |
